instructions:
First Phase 3 candidate from the rubric-extension thread. Build an LLM-as-judge harness that scores doc pages on the Clarity dimension (1-5 per rubric.json's existing 5-level definition). Pilot on 10 cohort-balanced pages — calibrate before scaling to other 4 dimensions (Completeness / Cross-platform parity / Examples / AI-consumability) or other pages.

New subdirectory: `.docs-ops/rubric-scorer/`. Designed to be DIMENSION-AGNOSTIC — adding the next dimension is just a new prompt file + same harness.

## Part A — Architecture

```
.docs-ops/rubric-scorer/
  README.md                  ← what this is, how to run, API key requirement
  score.py                   ← main scorer: reads page, calls LLM, writes scores
  render-report.py           ← turn scores JSON into markdown report
  pages-pilot.json           ← the 10 pilot pages (cohort-balanced)
  prompts/
    clarity.md               ← Clarity dimension prompt (template)
    # future: completeness.md, parity.md, examples.md, ai-consumability.md
  results/
    clarity-latest.json      ← per-page scores from most recent run
    clarity-latest-report.md ← human-readable rendering
    history/                 ← optional: <YYYY-MM-DD>-clarity.json snapshots
```

## Part B — Page selection (pilot, 10 pages)

Use `.docs-ops/integration-tests/pages.json` as input — already cohort-balanced. Pick the top 10 by a clear criterion (suggested: top 4 Eastern / top 4 Western / top 2 Shared, all from the high-traffic set). Document the selection rationale.

## Part C — Clarity prompt design (`prompts/clarity.md`)

The prompt should:
1. Quote the EXACT Clarity rubric definition from `.docs-ops/rubric.json` (dimensions.clarity.levels) — including the 1-5 level descriptions verbatim. Don't paraphrase; the rubric IS the contract.
2. Give the LLM the page's full content.
3. Ask for a score (1-5) with:
   - One-sentence rationale (cite specific evidence — line/section reference)
   - Confidence: low/medium/high
   - 2-3 concrete suggestions for improvement (only if score < 5)
4. Format response as strict JSON for parseability.

Design principle — make the prompt CONSERVATIVE: when unsure, default to lower score with reasoning. Better to flag a borderline page than to over-grade.

Prompt template idea (Copilot, iterate on this):

```
You are scoring a documentation page on the CLARITY dimension of a 6-dimension rubric.

Rubric (verbatim from .docs-ops/rubric.json):
  Level 1: <text>
  Level 2: <text>
  ...
  Level 5: <text>

PAGE (markdown, full content below):
---
{page_content}
---

Score the page on the Clarity dimension. Respond with JSON only:

{
  "score": <int 1-5>,
  "rationale": "<one sentence with specific evidence>",
  "confidence": "low|medium|high",
  "suggestions": ["<concrete improvement>", ...]  // only if score < 5; omit if score == 5
}
```

## Part D — Scorer (`score.py`)

CLI: `python3 score.py --dimension clarity [--pages pages-pilot.json] [--out results/clarity-latest.json]`.

Reads each page, builds the prompt by substituting rubric levels + page content, calls the LLM (default: Anthropic API via `anthropic` SDK; env var `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Parses response as JSON. Aggregates into per-page records.

Output schema (`results/clarity-latest.json`):

```json
{
  "generated_at": "<iso>",
  "dimension": "clarity",
  "rubric_version": "0.1.0",
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",  // or whichever model was used
  "pages_scored": 10,
  "avg_score": 4.2,
  "distribution": { "1": 0, "2": 0, "3": 2, "4": 5, "5": 3 },
  "by_page": [
    {
      "page": "social-plus-sdk/getting-started/authentication.mdx",
      "cohort": "shared",
      "score": 4,
      "rationale": "Solid section structure but the 'Auth Token' explanation runs 3 paragraphs before showing a concrete example (line 67-89).",
      "confidence": "high",
      "suggestions": ["Lead the Auth Token section with the code example, follow with explanation.", "Cut the Mermaid diagram intro paragraph."]
    }
  ]
}
```

Cost control: pilot is 10 pages × one LLM call each = ~50K input tokens total. Cheap. Document approximate cost in README.

Error handling: if LLM call fails or returns malformed JSON, record per-page failure with the raw response for debugging — don't crash the whole run.

## Part E — Renderer (`render-report.py`)

Reads `results/clarity-latest.json`, emits markdown to `results/clarity-latest-report.md`. Sections:
- Headline: avg score + distribution histogram (ASCII)
- Per-page table sorted lowest-score-first (so the action items rise to the top)
- Top suggestions across pages (deduplicated themes)

Keep simple — plain markdown.

## Part F — README

Brief: what this is, how to run (`export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... && python3 score.py --dimension clarity`), how to add a new dimension (drop a `prompts/<name>.md`, run `--dimension <name>`), pilot scope rationale, future plans.

## Part G — First run

RUN the scorer on the 10 pilot pages. Render the report. Don't iterate on prompt — just produce the first scores. The next round will look at the scores, spot-check 2-3 against human judgment (Claude can do this via spot-read), and refine the prompt.

## Out of scope

- Other dimensions — Clarity first; pattern proven before scaling.
- CI gate integration — scoring-as-gate is a much later step (needs calibration confidence first).
- LLM model comparison — pick Anthropic Claude (Sonnet 4.6 or Haiku 4.5 — your call; document which) and stick with it for v1.
- Cross-page comparison / trend analysis — single-snapshot v1; trend layer comes after multiple runs accumulate.

## Success criteria (LOOSE)

- Harness runs end-to-end on all 10 pilot pages without crashing.
- Each page has a valid score (1-5), rationale, confidence.
- `clarity-latest-report.md` renders cleanly.
- Cost: under $1 for the pilot run (Anthropic API).

Report in `notes`: avg score; distribution; 3 lowest-scoring pages with rationales (these are the immediate action items if scores look fair); your honest read on whether the LLM seems to be scoring consistently or wildly variable across pages.

validators.name: scorer_runs_and_produces_valid_clarity_report
validators.command: test -f .docs-ops/rubric-scorer/results/clarity-latest.json && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/rubric-scorer/results/clarity-latest.json')); assert d.get('dimension')=='clarity'; assert d.get('pages_scored',0)>=8, f'pages_scored {d.get(\"pages_scored\")} (expected >=8; allow 1-2 failures)'; assert all(1<=p.get('score',0)<=5 for p in d.get('by_page',[]) if 'score' in p), 'invalid scores'; print('OK')"
